title: Capsule capture metadata (meta.json)
description: Per-capture forensic record. Source of truth for the integrity story; signed by the case keypair. Schema v2 (hardening pass) adds tab_context, capture-side mutations (blocking + banner hide), render-wait outcomes, and cookie-set provenance hash. Schema v3 (Track A) adds the per-item manifest PDF artifact role + checksum, plus capture.report_lang for the locale the manifest PDF was rendered in. Schema v4 adds the per-item report PDF (``report_pdf`` artifact role) — a human-readable companion to the manifest PDF carrying provenance, full description, tools, and the capture report; its hash is bound to meta.json by the same signature. Schema v5 adds ``url_canonical`` (canonical form of url_final used for dedup hashing) and ``force_recapture_index`` (set when the row was an intentional re-capture per CLAUDE.md §15; null otherwise). Schema v6 adds the ``gallery`` capture_kind for image-gallery captures (gallery-dl producer): ``gallery_count`` and ``gallery_extractor`` at the root, ``gallery_dl_version`` under tools, ``gallery_NNN`` / ``gallery_NNN_meta`` / ``gallery_info`` artifact roles, and ``capture.gallery_attempted`` / ``capture.gallery_outcome`` for the capture report. Schema v7 (page-preservation hardening pass) adds in-session WARC provenance (``capture.warc.*``), animation-freeze and video-pause counters, console + HAR sidecars (``page_console`` and ``page_har`` artifact roles), the navigation response block (``capture.response``), the media-context viewport screenshot (``page_context_screenshot`` role), and ``warcio_version`` / ``browsertrix_version: null`` for in-session captures.

url_canonical.description: Canonical form of url_final used to derive url_hash for dedup (CLAUDE.md §15). Tracking params stripped, scheme/host lowercased, fragment dropped, trailing slash normalized, remaining query keys sorted. v5.

artifacts.description: Artifact role → relative path under /downloads. Roles include page_mhtml, page_screenshot, page_warc, user_browser_*, plus tab_context / session_state / dom_snapshot_* (v2). v3 adds ``manifest_pdf`` — the per-item evidence manifest PDF; its hash is bound to meta.json via the signature. v4 adds ``report_pdf`` — the human-readable per-item report PDF (provenance, description, tools, capture report); its hash is bound the same way. v6 adds gallery roles: ``gallery_NNN`` (one per image, NNN is a 1-based 3-digit zero-padded index), ``gallery_NNN_meta`` (gallery-dl per-image JSON sidecar), ``gallery_info`` (gallery-level info.json from gallery-dl). v7 adds ``page_har`` (Playwright HAR sidecar with sensitive headers redacted), ``page_console`` (JSON of browser console + pageerror events captured during the session), and ``page_context_screenshot`` (viewport-sized PNG framed on the most prominent <video>/<iframe> for media-in-context evidence).

browsertrix_version.description: browsertrix-crawler version when the browsertrix subprocess fallback ran (capture.warc.captured_in_session = false). ``null`` or ``"0"`` when the in-session WARC writer succeeded and browsertrix wasn't invoked. Schema-required for back-compat with v2–v6 records that always populated this field.

warcio_version.description: warcio version when the in-session CDP→WARC writer ran (capture.warc.captured_in_session = true). ``null`` when warcio is unavailable or the subprocess fallback ran. v7.

gallery_dl_version.description: gallery-dl version when gallery-dl ran (capture_kind == 'gallery') or was attempted. ``null`` for captures that never invoked gallery-dl (every video URL). v6.

capture.description: Capture-side mutations and render-wait outcomes (schema v2). Every blocked request and every banner CSS-hide is recorded here; the underlying WARC still contains the request attempts as aborted, so forensic completeness is preserved.

blocked_requests_sample.description: Up to 200 URLs of blocked requests. Full list is in the WARC.

report_lang.description: UI locale active at submission time. The per-item manifest PDF was rendered in this locale (RTL/LTR direction, label set, font fallbacks). v3.

gallery_attempted.description: True iff gallery-dl was invoked for this URL (i.e. yt-dlp returned no media and gallery-dl was enabled for the case). False otherwise. v6.

gallery_outcome.description: Outcome of the gallery-dl invocation, if attempted. ``captured`` means at least one image was retained; ``skipped`` means gallery-dl was disabled for this case. v6.

lazy_promoted_count.description: Number of /

lazy_load_max_height_px.description: Final document.scrollHeight after the lazy-load gate ran. Surfaces how tall the page actually rendered to. v7.

videos_paused.description: Count of autoplaying <video> elements that were paused before the still PNG was captured. v7.

animations_frozen.description: True iff the animation-freeze CSS layer was injected before the screenshot (and removed after). MHTML and WARC are captured before the freeze and are unaffected. v7.

shadow_dom_walked.description: True iff the visible-images render-wait gate traversed shadow roots. v7.

iframes_seen.description: Count of iframe/frame elements detected after lazy-load — includes both same-origin and cross-origin frames. v7.

screenshot_truncated_at_px.description: When the page exceeded DEFAULT_SCREENSHOT_MAX_HEIGHT_PX (30,000px), the still PNG was clipped at this height. ``null`` for normal full-page captures. MHTML and WARC are never clipped. v7.

readiness_timed_out.description: True iff the outer 60s render-wait budget was exceeded and remaining gates were skipped. v7.

console_message_count.description: Count of browser console events captured during the session (every console.log/warn/error/info, plus pageerror exceptions). Full event list lives in the page_console artifact. v7.

console_error_count.description: Subset of console_message_count counting pageerror exceptions only. v7.

response.description: Navigation response block — final URL, status, redirect chain, sanitized headers (Cookie/Set-Cookie/Authorization stripped), and elapsed_ms. v7.

media_context_captured.description: True iff a viewport-sized screenshot was captured framed on the most prominent <video>/<iframe> on the page. The artifact lives at the page_context_screenshot role. v7.

media_context_selector.description: CSS selector of the element the media-context screenshot was framed on. Recorded for forensic clarity. v7.

warc.description: WARC session provenance. captured_in_session = true means the WARC was written by the same Playwright session that produced MHTML+PNG (CDP→WARC writer); false means the browsertrix-crawler subprocess fallback ran. v7.

encoding_normalized.description: True iff response bodies were normalized to Content-Encoding: identity (CDP returns decoded bodies). False for browsertrix subprocess captures (those record on-the-wire bytes).

stalled_count.description: Number of stall events the runner emitted during this capture (no progress JSON for >= STALL_THRESHOLD_S). Mirrors download_options.stalled_count. v8.

cookies_snapshot_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the cookies file the job consumed. Binds the capture to the exact cookie set without ever logging values. v2.

ephemeral_cookies_used.description: True iff the job used a one-shot ephemeral cookie file (extension-supplied, never persisted to the case directory). v2.

force_recapture_index.description: Set when this row was an intentional re-capture per CLAUDE.md §15 (the user clicked 'Re-capture as new entry' on a duplicate-detection modal). Index 1 is implicit for the original; 2, 3, … name successive forced re-captures. ``null`` for normal captures. v5.

gallery_count.description: Number of images preserved when capture_kind == 'gallery' (one entry per ``gallery_NNN`` artifact). ``null`` for non-gallery kinds. v6.

gallery_extractor.description: gallery-dl ``category`` (its extractor identifier, lower-case — e.g. ``pixiv``, ``twitter``, ``imgur``) for the gallery this row preserves. ``null`` for non-gallery kinds. v6.

download_options.description: Investigator-facing download knobs (CLAUDE.md §15 v0.7). Always emitted on v8+ records so absence-vs-default is unambiguous; older records (v2–v7) omit this block. The signature on meta.json transitively binds these values.

audio_only.description: True iff yt-dlp ran with -x and the downloaded artifact is the extracted audio stream. The page snapshot (MHTML/PNG/WARC) still preserves the original video; this flag is the unambiguous record that the on-disk media is audio-only. v8.

quality_cap.description: Per-job format cap that overrode the profile default. ``audio`` is equivalent to audio_only=true; ``480``/``720``/``1080`` cap height with bestvideo+bestaudio fallback; ``best`` lifts any profile-imposed cap. ``null`` ⇒ profile default. v8.

subtitle_langs.description: BCP-47-ish language tags requested via --write-subs --sub-langs. The literal ``all`` sentinel maps to ``all,-live_chat``. Empty ⇒ no subtitles requested. v8.

restart_count.description: Number of times the user clicked Restart (--no-continue + .part wipe) on this job before it finalized. 0 for jobs that succeeded on the first attempt. v8.
